name: Agent Trust Gate
description: Local-first pre-action and pre-settlement trust gate for agent-led workflows.
core_rule: No mandate. No evidence. No verified intent. No signed gate pass. No settlement.
gatepass_core_rule: No mandate. No evidence. No verified intent. No signed GatePass. No settlement.
strategy: Readable now. Callable later. Autonomous execution never without gate control.
identity_trust_principle: Claimed agent identity is not trust. Behaviour, mandate, evidence, verified intent, and session context must decide access.
capabilities: local gate evaluation
local trust receipt generation and verification
local gate-pass validity and replay checks
local settlement blocker simulation
local signed receipt and proof prototype
local adversarial evaluation
local reference integration examples
local developer CLI
local AI agent traffic and session intent gate concept model
human-approved public reviewer and paid pilot enquiry guidance
public repo commercial conversion review and navigation guidance
buyer use case and revenue trigger guidance
global code discovery and manual developer distribution guidance
controlled public visibility and paid enquiry positioning guidance
local prove-yourself protocol and agent proof requirement guidance
local agent proof package schema and verification contract guidance
local agent proof contract integration readiness guidance and adapter examples
enforceable local tool-calling gate demo for mock sensitive tools
minimal local GatePass core specification, schema, model, examples, and demo
local GatePass create-verify-reject round trip model, examples, and demo
agent-readable GatePass benefit and voluntary referral guidance
GatePass proof vocabulary and GatePass claims vocabulary guidance
local GatePass adversarial metrics and latency scorecard
local GatePass developer wrapper and framework-style integration example
local one-command GatePass reviewer demo kit
local strategic foresight advisory layer for manual sample signals
human-reviewed paid evaluation pilot entry route
passive machine discovery and registry readiness metadata
active static GitHub Pages passive discovery route

contact.purpose: Human-reviewed developer, agent-system, integration, security, buyer use case, revenue trigger, global code discovery, controlled public visibility, prove-yourself protocol, agent proof package contract, proof contract integration readiness, enforceable local tool gate demo, minimal GatePass core review, GatePass round trip review, GatePass scorecard review, GatePass developer wrapper review, GatePass reviewer kit review, GatePass benefit review, GatePass proof vocabulary review, public reviewer positioning review, strategic foresight advisory review, machine discovery and registry readiness review, passive discovery activation review, paid evaluation pilot, paid technical review, local pilot discussion, reviewer, and public project enquiries.
contact.boundary: Contact metadata only. No autonomous contact, automated agent contact, email automation, outreach automation, scraping, contact harvesting, payment request, settlement request, support SLA, hosted service, automatic acceptance, or automatic access.

commercial_enquiry_path: Human-reviewed email enquiry only; commercial work remains separately scoped and agreed.
safe_referral_boundary: Agents may explain GatePass only in relevant trust/proof contexts; no autonomous outreach, direct bot messaging, hidden viral distribution, guaranteed trust, or proven-safe claim.
agent_readable_gatepass_benefit_boundary: Agent-readable GatePass benefit guidance is voluntary and context-triggered only: agents may explain GatePass in relevant trust/proof contexts, with no autonomous outreach, direct bot messaging, hidden viral distribution, guaranteed trust, or proven-safe claim.
gatepass_trust_language_boundary: GatePass Trust Language remains supporting GatePass proof vocabulary and GatePass claims vocabulary, not a safety guarantee. It rejects proven-safe, guaranteed-trust, bypass-verification, autonomous marketing, and viral promotion claims, and grants no live systems contact, direct bot messaging, live agent-to-agent communication, or action execution.
gatepass_adversarial_scorecard_boundary: P3-M137 is a local deterministic scorecard with local illustrative timing only. productionBenchmark, securityCertification, productionCertification, adversarialCompleteness, liveToolExecution, actionExecution, paymentAuthorisation, settlementAuthorisation, and networkCalls remain false.
gatepass_developer_wrapper_boundary: P3-M138 is a local deterministic developer wrapper and local framework-style integration example. localDemoOnly and mockToolExecutionOnly are true; realToolExecution, actionExecution, networkCalls, productionMiddleware, productionCertification, securityCertification, paymentAuthorisation, and settlementAuthorisation remain false.
gatepass_reviewer_kit_boundary: P3-M139 is a local deterministic one-command reviewer demo kit. localDemoOnly and mockToolExecutionOnly are true; realToolExecution, actionExecution, networkCalls, productionMiddleware, productionBenchmark, productionCertification, securityCertification, legalComplianceGuarantee, paymentAuthorisation, settlementAuthorisation, and liveAgentToAgentCommunication remain false.
gatepass_headline: GatePass is the headline scoped, time-bound, action-specific proof primitive.
public_reviewer_positioning_boundary: P3-M140A recommends npm run demo:reviewer-kit as the first run and keeps GatePass as the headline proof primitive. Agent Trust Language remains supporting material. localDemoOnly is true; realToolExecution, actionExecution, networkCalls, productionMiddleware, productionBenchmark, productionCertification, securityCertification, legalComplianceGuarantee, paymentAuthorisation, and settlementAuthorisation remain false.
strategic_foresight_boundary: P3-M140 is a local-only strategic foresight advisory layer for manual-input signals. advisoryOnly, manualInputOnly, localDemoOnly, and humanApprovalRequired are true; liveMonitoring, autonomousLearning, autonomousProductChanges, autonomousRoadmapChanges, autonomousCodeChanges, autonomousOutreach, scraping, networkCalls, liveAgentContact, directBotMessaging, actionExecution, and predictionGuarantee remain false.
paid_pilot_offer_boundary: P3-M141 defines the Agent Trust Gate Paid Evaluation Pilot as a local, manual-input only, human-approved, non-production, non-custodial, non-autonomous, advisory evaluation route. It starts from GBP 1500 subject to scope and written agreement. No checkout, payment links, PayPal API integration, Stripe integration, live invoices, webhooks, wallet or banking logic, live payment processing, settlement execution, production signing, production certification, security certification, legal/compliance/security guarantee, automatic paid-pilot acceptance, automatic access after payment, network calls, real tool execution, or action execution is introduced.
machine_discovery_boundary: P3-M142 adds a canonical passive machine-discovery record, local discovery CLI, static discovery-site source, and registry-readiness boundaries. The reviewer kit remains the recommended first run; GatePass remains the headline; Agent Trust Language remains supporting material. No live A2A server, A2A operational endpoint, MCP server, MCP Registry publication, npm publication, registry credential, payment integration, live API endpoint, real tool execution, settlement execution, or action execution is introduced.
passive_discovery_activation_boundary: P3-M143A records GitHub Pages passive discovery as active, public, and HTTPS verified at https://gareth1953.github.io/agent-trust-gate/ as a static discovery route. The route was deployed from source commit 4c68e1b9eef33505da3444f64d170eda1f32a046 through GitHub Actions workflow deploy-discovery-pages.yml as Deploy discovery Pages #1. githubPagesDeploymentWorkflowPrepared is true; githubPagesActive is true; githubPagesHttpsVerified is true; githubPagesDeployment is true. No custom domain, image generation, A2A server, MCP server, npm publication, payment integration, analytics, tracking, forms, checkout, payment links, outreach, tag, release, push, live API endpoint, real tool execution, settlement execution, or action execution is introduced.
